title: dryopteris hololepis
intro: Dryopteris hololepis, also known as the Hawaiian shield fern, is a species of fern native to the Hawaiian Islands, characterized by its leathery fronds and robust growth habit. The plant has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Dryopteris hololepis are said to include reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating skin conditions, as well as providing antioxidant and immune-boosting properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and salves, and may be combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
